content: The radius of the Earth is 6400 km . If the height of an antenna is 500 m , then its range is
explanation:
To find the range of the antenna (i.e., the distance to the horizon), we can use the formula:

$$d = \sqrt{2Rh}$$

where:

• $$R$$ is the radius of the Earth

• $$h$$ is the height of the antenna

Since the Earth's radius is given as 6400 km and the antenna's height is 500 m, we first convert the height into kilometers:

• $$500 \text{ m} = 0.5 \text{ km}$$

Now, plug the values into the formula:

$$d = \sqrt{2 \times 6400 \times 0.5} = \sqrt{6400} = 80 \text{ km}$$

Thus, the range of the antenna is 80 km, which corresponds to Option C.

content:
A basic communication system consists of
(i) Transmitter
(ii) Information sources
(iii) User of information
(iv) Channel
(v) Receiver

options.content: ii, i, iv, v and iii
ii, v, i, iv and iii
ii, iv, i, iii and v
